{{Quote|We've had our losses lately. Terris and Naima|Amie Antin|<ref name="Secret weapon">''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]''</ref>}}
'''Naima''' was a [[Bellassan|native]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bellassa/Legends|Bellassa]] who was a member of [[the Eleven]] resistance group. Around [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]], while Naima and fellow resistance member [[Terris]] were [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] [[Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing starfighter/Legends|V-wing starfighters]], they encountered an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Unidentified Imperial starship (Bellassa)|starship]] above Bellassa. The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] ordered the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] to fire on them, and both Naima and Terris were [[Death/Legends|killed]]. Later, [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Amie Antin]] told resistance members [[Roan Lands]] and [[Dona Telamark]] of Naima's passing.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Lord Vader, we have starfighters on our radar not registered to the Bellassan government. Possible members of the resistance.|An Imperial captain, to Darth Vader|<ref name="Secret weapon">''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]''</ref>}}
[[File:FerusOlin-LotJ4.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ferus Olin (pictured)  was a friend of Naima and witnessed her death.]]
Naima was a [[Bellassan|native]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bellassa/Legends|Bellassa]] who was a member of [[the Eleven]] resistance group. She<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> was a friend of the resistance members [[Ferus Olin]]<ref name="Secret weapon" /> and [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Amie Antin]].<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> Around [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> Naima and fellow resistance member [[Terris]] were [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] [[Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing starfighter/Legends|V-wing starfighters]] when they encountered an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Unidentified Imperial starship (Bellassa)|starship]] near Bellassa's inner [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. The [[Starship/Legends|vessel's]] [[Unidentified Imperial captain (Bellassa)|captain]] reported to<ref name="Secret weapon" /> the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]]<ref name="Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] that the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] were on their [[Radar/Legends|radar]] and that they were not registered to Bellassa's government, meaning they were possible resistance members. After the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] told Vader that the starfighters had not identified themselves, Vader ordered them to blast the fighters. Although the captain protested, stating they were probably just doing surveillance, they eventually complied, destroying one of the fighters. As the remaining fighter attempted to avoid the Imperial starship, an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (Bellassa)|officer]] commented on the way it squirmed. The V-wing was destroyed soon after.<ref name="Secret weapon" />

Olin, who was acting as a [[Spy/Legends|double-agent]] aboard the Imperial starship, felt helpless watching as the fighters were destroyed and thought it was likely he either knew them or their friends or family. Later, on Bellassa, Antin told resistance members [[Roan Lands]] and [[Dona Telamark]] about the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of Naima and Terris. Lands was saddened by their deaths and hoped Olin had not known the starfighters were piloted by friends.<ref name="Secret weapon" />

==Personality and traits==
Although he was unaware of her identity, Ferus Olin believed that Naima was brave to attack an Imperial starship.<ref name="Secret weapon" />

==Equipment==
Naima piloted a V-wing starfighter.<ref name="Secret weapon" />

==Behind the scenes==
Naima first indirectly appeared in a starfighter in ''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]'', a [[2007]] junior novel written by [[Jude Watson]] as the seventh installment in the ''[[Star Wars: The Last of the Jedi]]'' series.<ref name="Secret weapon" />
